strengths: Deep TypeScript and Node.js expertise across multiple senior roles spanning 6+ years, directly matching the primary stack.
Hands-on AI agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic tooling) aligns well with the AI agent development requirement.
Proven AWS and cloud infrastructure experience at Optilyz (AWS CDK) combined with Docker usage across several roles covers the cloud/containerization requirements.
concerns: No Nest.js anywhere on the resume despite it being the primary backend framework in the JD, and the candidate's Node.js experience is split across raw Express-style patterns, GraphQL, and AWS CDK abstractions — there is no evidence of the opinionated, decorator-driven architecture Nest.js demands, meaning ramp-up risk is high on day one of production work.

A ~9-month career gap (October 2025 – July 2026) immediately follows the candidate's most recent role, which was itself only 6 months long (re:cinq, April–October 2025); this back-to-back pattern of a short tenure into a prolonged gap raises questions about sustained engagement and leaves the candidate's current technical currency unverified at a time when the AI/cloud stack is moving fast.

The JD requires TypeScript, Kotlin/Java, and Python proficiency across the full stack, but the resume lists no Python whatsoever, no Kotlin, and Java last appears circa 2016–2018 on robotic-arm firmware at Altom — not backend services — meaning two of the three required secondary languages are either absent or nine-plus years stale, leaving the candidate materially underqualified on the polyglot backend requirement.

key_factors.factor: 9-month career gap (Oct 2025 – Jul 2026) with no explained activity
No Nest.js, Vitest, Playwright, Python, or Kotlin — multiple required stack items absent
Genuine AI agent infra experience (re:cinq MCP, TypeScript/Node.js depth)

response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's resume has meaningful gaps against the core requirements: Nest.js, the primary backend framework in the JD, appears nowhere on his resume, and his Node.js experience spans raw Express-style patterns, GraphQL, and CDK abstractions rather than the opinionated, decorator-driven architecture Nest.js demands, creating immediate day-one ramp-up risk. The JD's polyglot backend requirement (TypeScript, Kotlin/Java, Python) is materially unmet — Python is entirely absent, Kotlin never appears, and Java was last used circa 2016–2018 on robotic-arm firmware, not backend services. Compounding these stack gaps is a ~9-month career gap running from October 2025 to July 2026, immediately following a 6-month tenure at re:cinq, leaving the candidate's technical currency unverified at a point when the AI/cloud toolchain is evolving rapidly. Taken together, the missing required technologies and the sustained unexplained gap make this candidate a weak fit for a mid-senior IC role where immediate production contribution is expected.
